checks.a_analysis: The output reflects the content found in the source document without inventing new body text, introductions, or conclusions. The placeholder note regarding omitted sections is an expected technical metadata element from the conversion pipeline and not a fabrication of the document's content.
There are no phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs present in the source document or the output. Attendance tables use 'X' to denote status, which is correctly preserved.

The source PDF consists of 13 pages. The output is missing two entire pages of content: Page 7, which contains the 'clean' version of the Emergency Medical Dispatch and Telecommunicator Training Requirements, and Page 9, which contains the 'Evolving to NG9-1-1' infographic and architecture diagram. The JSON output acknowledges this omission with a placeholder: '{"_omitted": "2 sections omitted for token budget"}'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 7: 'Emergency Medical Dispatch (EMD)... Telecommunicator Training Requirements...'; Page 9: 'Evolving to NG9-1-1 enables NC to support the changing needs of its citizens... NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture'

checks.a_analysis: All tables in the processed sections (Agenda, Minutes, Survey Results) contain the correct number of rows as per the source PDF. For example, the committee member roll call vote on page 3 contains 6 rows, which matches the output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Within the sections that were successfully converted, no significant text omissions were found. The redlined text on Page 8 is correctly captured with formatting properties (strikethrough and underline) identifying the changes.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording, dates, and names in the converted sections faithfully represent the source material. Meaning-altering changes were not observed.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire document and compared it with the source PDF. Content repetition (such as the NC DIT logo and document titles) occurs in the source at the start of distinct documents (Agenda vs. Minutes) and is correctly reflected in the output sections. There are no instances of paragraphs or list items being duplicated incorrectly in the conversion process.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows the document's structure. Major sections use a 'title' field, while subsections (such as the Training Syllabus and Survey Results) utilize H2 and H3 tags correctly. For example, in the survey results section (index 7), questions are marked as H2 and 'Other Responses' is an H3, which is correct nesting. No heading levels are skipped.

The tables are well-represented. A complex layout on page 2, which features two side-by-side tables (Committee Members and Guests), has been correctly linearized into a single 7-column table in Section 2, preserving the horizontal relationship between members and guests. The staff attendance table is appended to this structure, which is a reasonable representation of the combined 'Meeting Attendees' section. Vote tables and survey result tables match the column counts and headers of the source precisely.

The reading order matches the source PDF exactly, progressing from the Agenda to the Minutes, then the Training Syllabus, Policy Drafts, Survey Results, and finally the Committee Goals. Elements within sections (such as tables following the paragraphs that introduce them) are in the correct sequence.

Lists are correctly identified and formatted as either 'ordered' or 'unordered'. Numbered agenda items in the minutes and the goals section are converted to ordered lists. Multi-level nesting in the Training Syllabus (Section 3) and regulatory requirements (Section 5) accurately reflects the source hierarchy.
The source document contains no active hyperlinks. The output correctly reflects this by not generating any fabricated link elements.

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual organization of the HTML faithfully follows the logical flow of the source PDF. Document sections, including the agenda, minutes, training outlines, and survey results, are presented in the same order. Although the HTML uses a responsive single-column layout, the relationship between headings, text, and tables remains clear and consistent with the source document's intent. The complex redlined content on page 8 is effectively reorganized into a text section followed by a table of comments, which is an appropriate visual adaptation for web accessibility.

All tables from the source PDF, including the meeting attendees list (page 2), the roll call vote tables (pages 3-4), and the website survey results (pages 10-12), are correctly rendered as HTML tables. Columns and rows are properly aligned, and headers are visually distinct. The data remains readable and understandable.

A significant and meaningful infographic is missing from the HTML output. On page 9 of the source PDF, the section 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' is followed by a large, complex diagram illustrating the call flow and architecture. In the rendered HTML, this heading appears, but no image or diagram follows it. The logos on pages 1 and 6 are correctly present.
checks.b_evidence_source: On page 9, there is a complex infographic/diagram titled 'NC NG911: Speedy call delivery with caller location and media' under the heading 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture'.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the heading 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' is present, but it is not followed by any image or diagram content; it is immediately followed by the next section header.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'Evolving to NG911', corresponding to PDF page 9.

checks.a_analysis: There are no instances of content blocks, tables, or sections being visually duplicated in the HTML output.
